title: Andrew

jokes.text: She wasn't mocking Europeans. I would say the song was jesty.
jokes.setup: Press asking about Selina's controversial song mocking Europeans
jokes.punchline: Mike's desperate invention of 'jesty' as a word to defend the indefensible
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Mike's PR desperation leads to inventing nonsensical words. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Okay, slow down, Leon. Is your blood sugar off? Have a pastry or some herring, okay?
jokes.setup: Leon pressing Mike with difficult questions about the spy situation
jokes.punchline: Mike's pathetic deflection by suggesting blood sugar issues and offering fish
jokes.explanation: Great character work - Mike's increasingly desperate PR deflection tactics. The herring detail is absurd and specific.

jokes.text: This isn't 'Meyer the Liar' again. Well, it is now.
jokes.setup: Mike trying to dismiss the 'Meyer the Liar' narrative
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself and admitting defeat
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - the immediate reversal is perfectly timed. Shows Mike's complete lack of control over the narrative.

jokes.text: How's Catherine? She's 21. That's great. Yeah, we're having her party tonight.
jokes.setup: Standard pleasantries about family
jokes.punchline: Selina's flat delivery of her daughter's age as if it's an accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor - Selina's detached parenting style showing through mundane conversation.

jokes.text: And stay out of Middle East politics. So two out of three ain't bad.
jokes.setup: Selina listing what she wants for Catherine: happy, healthy, and staying out of Middle East politics
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that her daughter has failed one of three basic parental wishes
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - treating Middle East involvement as equivalent to health concerns. Great character work showing Selina's skewed priorities.

jokes.text: I'm amazed you can laugh about that. Yes, that's what I do. I'm laughing about it.
jokes.setup: Mary expressing concern about Catherine's Middle East involvement
jokes.punchline: Selina's robotic confirmation that she's laughing, delivered without any humor
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Selina's mechanical response reveals she's not actually finding anything funny about her daughter's situation.

jokes.text: It's like waiting for a biopsy result.
jokes.setup: Amy wondering how the negotiations are going
jokes.punchline: Comparing political negotiations to cancer diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Sharp dark humor with excellent economy. The medical comparison captures the anxiety perfectly.

jokes.text: She did own Brian Earl in negotiations by pretending to faint.
jokes.setup: Amy explaining Mary's negotiating skills
jokes.punchline: Revealing that 'owning' someone involved theatrical medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Good setup for what's coming with Mary. Shows the absurd theatrical nature of DC politics.

jokes.text: Well, my mom doesn't live in Rome, but she's still a fucking Catholic.
jokes.setup: Sue claiming Ed doesn't count as DC because he works in Boston
jokes.punchline: Dan's crude analogy about geography not changing fundamental nature
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dan line - crude but clever analogy with perfect character voice. The profanity enhances rather than substitutes for the wit.

jokes.text: Oh, what do you want, a fucking cake? Look, I can be happy. See? There you go.
jokes.setup: Sue sarcastically noting Dan's happiness for her
jokes.punchline: Dan's performatively fake happiness demonstration
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Dan's inability to express genuine emotion, even when trying to fake it.

jokes.text: I think it's over. Sneezes always come in threes. Have you ever noticed that? Really? Is that so? Oh, well, that was a fourth. So maybe I need to tell you about how counting works, right?
jokes.setup: Mary claiming sneezes come in threes after her third sneeze
jokes.punchline: Immediately sneezing a fourth time, leading to Selina's cutting response about counting
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and reversal. Mary's false wisdom immediately undermined, followed by Selina's brutal put-down.

jokes.text: Gary, deflower the room. There's a sentence I bet you thought you'd never hear.
jokes.setup: Selina telling Gary to remove the flowers causing Mary's allergic reaction
jokes.punchline: Selina's self-aware commentary on the sexual connotation of 'deflower'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with meta-commentary. Shows Selina's wit even in crisis moments. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I'm not good for another four hours.
jokes.setup: Mary having an allergic reaction during negotiations
jokes.punchline: Her statement about not being 'good' sounds sexual in context
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo humor - works more as awkward character moment than strong joke.

jokes.text: Ugh, just because my last name rhymes with liar. How lucky am I that I'm not Selina Mapist or Selina Medophile?
jokes.setup: Selina complaining about 'Meyer the Liar' nickname
jokes.punchline: Imagining worse rhyming alternatives like 'rapist' and 'pedophile'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - takes the premise to its darkly logical extreme. Shows Selina's twisted perspective on her problems.

jokes.text: Jesus. You know, if we don't get this done by midnight, we're all gonna turn into ugly sisters and get eaten by wolves or whatever the fuck happens at the end of that story.
jokes.setup: Time pressure for completing the debt deal
jokes.punchline: Ben's confused fairy tale metaphor with profane uncertainty about the ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben character work - mixing high-pressure politics with mangled children's stories. The uncertainty about the ending makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Either way, just blur the fuck out of it.
jokes.setup: Ben laying out Selina's no-win PR situation with the hostage story
jokes.punchline: His solution is complete obfuscation rather than truth
jokes.explanation: Excellent character line - Ben's cynical pragmatism distilled to its essence. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: I think he's nervous. His voice sounded clammy.
jokes.setup: Sue reporting that Ed Webster is here to see Amy
jokes.punchline: Describing voice as 'clammy' instead of 'shaky' or similar
jokes.explanation: Good Sue line - her unique way of describing things. 'Clammy voice' is an impossible but vivid description.

jokes.text: It's good that you'll have sex soon.
jokes.setup: Amy looking flustered about her date
jokes.punchline: Selina's inappropriate boss commentary on Amy's sex life
jokes.explanation: Classic inappropriate Selina boss behavior. The directness and casualness make it cringe-funny.

jokes.text: Version two-- Mary King steals peace pipe, breaks it in half, shits all over it.
jokes.setup: Dan outlining two press release versions for the debt negotiations
jokes.punchline: Increasingly crude and violent imagery for the failure version
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character work for Dan's crude communication style. The imagery builds effectively.

jokes.text: I mean, he just fluffs ya, and then he fucks ya. Yeah. Well, speaking of... Oh, yeah, that's a perfect example.
jokes.setup: Selina describing Andrew's manipulative pattern
jokes.punchline: Gary's attempt to transition to a new topic gets interpreted as sexual
jokes.explanation: Mild sexual humor from miscommunication. Works as awkward character interaction.

jokes.text: Yeah, they had crazy intercourse.
jokes.setup: Selina describing Andrew taking her to Cap d'Antibes
jokes.punchline: Gary's clinical yet inappropriate description of their sex life
jokes.explanation: Gary's inappropriate oversharing and clinical language about his boss's sex life creates cringe humor.

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah. He's like a belly dancer with a hammer.
jokes.setup: Describing Andrew's manipulative seduction and betrayal pattern
jokes.punchline: Bizarre mixed metaphor combining graceful seduction with brutal violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist imagery - completely unexpected metaphor that somehow perfectly captures Andrew's seduction/betrayal pattern. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: She's calling him Andy instead of Andrew. It's starting.
jokes.setup: Selina using the nickname 'Andy'
jokes.punchline: Dan and Amy recognizing this as a tell for her emotional vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Good character observation - shows how well the staff knows Selina's patterns. Sets up her emotional vulnerability.

jokes.text: It's so weird. It's like a cat on a hot tin dog.
jokes.setup: Describing Selina's behavior around Andrew
jokes.punchline: Gary's mispronunciation of 'hot tin roof' as 'hot tin dog'
jokes.explanation: Classic Gary malapropism - creates absurd imagery while showing his character. The food reference makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I'm gonna be the sexiest woman to ever exude fiscal prudence. And you know what? That's a very fucking tough look to pull off.
jokes.setup: Selina preparing for the negotiation with Andrew
jokes.punchline: Combining sexuality with boring fiscal policy, then acknowledging the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of sexy and unsexy concepts. Selina's self-awareness about the contradiction makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Um, fair warning, I party pretty hard. You should see me dance. I can't be controlled. I do a lot of pelvic thrusts. Just very localized dancing. Very small orbits.
jokes.setup: Ed trying to sound cool and party-worthy
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and unsexy descriptions of his dancing style
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation - Ed's attempt at being impressive becomes increasingly pathetic and specific.

jokes.text: I was like the Hunter S. Thompson of bar mitzvahs.
jokes.setup: Ed claiming he's been 'wrecking parties since 13'
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to gonzo journalist at Jewish coming-of-age parties
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comparison - mixing counterculture icon with suburban Jewish celebrations. Highly original and quotable.

jokes.text: We-- we-- we-- we-- we met in... in Bumfuck, Idaho, I think it was. DC. Yes, yes. That's what I like to call DC as a joke.
jokes.setup: Selina not remembering where she met Ed
jokes.punchline: Her attempted recovery making it worse by calling DC 'Bumfuck Idaho'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Selina's attempt to recover from not remembering Ed makes things significantly worse.

jokes.text: Hey, buddy, don't worry that she didn't remember you, okay? It's only 'cause you're a nonentity.
jokes.setup: Dan consoling Ed about Selina not remembering him
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty disguised as comfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan character work - false sympathy that's actually more insulting. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: I mean, don't you think that you and your friends would call this whole thing an epic succeed?
jokes.setup: Selina showing off Catherine's birthday party
jokes.punchline: Using outdated/wrong slang 'epic succeed' instead of 'epic win'
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor - Selina trying to connect with youth culture and failing. Shows her disconnect from her daughter's world.

jokes.text: Remember that one time when you said you were way into 'Twilight' and I thought you meant the time of day? It really is the most dangerous time for traffic, though.
jokes.setup: Andrew remembering a specific conversation with Jenny about Twilight
jokes.punchline: Revealing he thought she meant the time of day, then doubling down on traffic safety
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character work - shows Andrew's superficial charm and genuine confusion about pop culture.

jokes.text: Mom, you've invited the House Majority Leader to my birthday party? Yeah, sweetheart, it's the 100 billion cutoff. You've heard about that? Of course I've heard about that.
jokes.setup: Catherine's reasonable complaint about work invading her birthday
jokes.punchline: Selina treating Catherine like she should understand high-level politics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's complete inability to separate work from personal life, even at her daughter's birthday.

jokes.text: Don't you think that you and your friends would call this whole thing an epic succeed?
jokes.setup: Selina trying to connect with Catherine about the party
jokes.punchline: Mangling youth slang with 'epic succeed'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe - Selina trying to be cool with young people but getting the slang wrong. Very parental.

jokes.text: Thank you, Rahim. You drink alcohol? Yes, ma'am. Oh, you Iranians are full of surprises.
jokes.setup: Rahim bringing Selina champagne
jokes.punchline: Selina's surprised reaction to Iranian drinking alcohol, revealing her stereotyping
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's casual stereotyping and inappropriate boss behavior. The surprise reveals her assumptions.

jokes.text: Some gibberish. I can't understand him.
jokes.setup: Rahim saying 'Salâmati' (cheers in Persian)
jokes.punchline: Selina dismissing his language as 'gibberish'
jokes.explanation: Character-revealing but not particularly clever. Shows Selina's cultural insensitivity more than wit.

jokes.text: You plus 10'd yourself here? Number crunchers to help you finish the deal. Yeah. And one of them is a designated driver because I am going to get fucked up.
jokes.setup: Selina questioning Ben bringing extra people to the party
jokes.punchline: Ben's casual announcement of his intention to get very drunk at a work function
jokes.explanation: Good Ben character work - his blunt honesty about planning to get drunk while working. Shows his cynical approach to everything.

jokes.text: You look so good, you're like a work of art. You make this stuff look like shit. Compared to you, that Monet is a piece of shit.
jokes.setup: Andrew complimenting Selina at the party
jokes.punchline: Escalating to calling priceless art 'shit' by comparison
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Andrew's manipulative charm. The absurdity of insulting Monet makes it funny.

jokes.text: Feeling up all the mermaids. They should cover up if they don't want it. If they don't want action, they shouldn't advertise.
jokes.setup: Andrew talking about scuba diving with coral and fish
jokes.punchline: Sexual harassment logic applied to mythical sea creatures
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist humor showing Andrew's character. The victim-blaming applied to fictional beings is darkly funny but reveals his mindset.

jokes.text: What are you doing? Are you gonna propose? What would your answer be? My answer would be Jesus fucking Christ.
jokes.setup: Andrew kneeling or in a proposal-like position
jokes.punchline: Selina's profane rejection of hypothetical proposal
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing their toxic dynamic. Selina's immediate horror at the prospect is well-delivered.

jokes.text: Well, technically, ma'am, he had the palm of your hand in his hand.
jokes.setup: Selina claiming she had Andrew 'in the palm of her hand'
jokes.punchline: Gary's pedantic correction about who was actually holding whose hand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary pedantry - his literal interpretation undermines Selina's attempt to claim control. Good structure and character integration.

jokes.text: Gary, you just called me sweetie. Oh, my God! I'm sorry. That's what I call Dana. Did you ever call Dana ma'am? I did once and it was awful.
jokes.setup: Gary accidentally using intimate language with his boss
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he's mixing up boss and girlfriend terms
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor about blurred boundaries. Gary's over-intimate relationship with his boss creates awkward comedy.

jokes.text: If I were drunk right now, would you kiss me? Yeah. No, Gary, I'm kidding. Right. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Selina asking Gary a hypothetical about kissing
jokes.punchline: Gary's immediate 'yes' followed by awkward backpedaling
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable boss-employee boundary crossing. Gary's honest answer creates awkwardness.

jokes.text: Something sex my dick trick? Did you say dick? Sex your dick trick. I don't understand what you just said.
jokes.setup: Mary trying to communicate while drunk/impaired
jokes.punchline: Incomprehensible sexual gibberish that sounds inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Weak drunk humor relying on nonsense words that sound sexual. Not particularly clever or well-structured.

jokes.text: You know, you look great in an art gallery 'cause you kind of are a work of art. I mean, I know I'd like to nail you up against a wall. No, that was knowing. I was being knowing.
jokes.setup: Andrew complimenting Selina using art metaphors
jokes.punchline: Accidental sexual innuendo followed by claiming it was intentional wordplay
jokes.explanation: Decent double entendre with art hanging/sexual reference. Andrew's attempt to cover shows his character.

jokes.text: I cannot stand teenage girls. Were you ever one? Never. Me neither.
jokes.setup: Complaining about noise from teenage girls
jokes.punchline: Both claiming they were never teenage girls
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment showing both women's disdain for typical feminine behavior, even their own past.

jokes.text: I wonder how much vodka we'd need to have to have a little Mrs. Robinson moment. That is extremely disrespectful. What are you talking about? That's like the highest compliment you can give somebody.
jokes.setup: Mike making sexual comments about Selina
jokes.punchline: His defense that sexual objectification is a compliment
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate humor that reveals Mike's character flaws. The cultural reference works but the overall joke is weak.

jokes.text: Yeah, antibiotics. Just keeping fresh. I'm not mentally ill if that's what you're implying.
jokes.setup: Ed asking if Jonah is on medication after his inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Jonah's bizarre logic about antibiotics and defensive mental health denial
jokes.explanation: Good Jonah character work - his weird logic and defensiveness. The 'keeping fresh' phrase is oddly specific.

jokes.text: And I think sometimes we need to just take time. Not long at all. I mean, maybe 10 minutes or-- okay, or maybe 15 is also good. I'm gonna suggest that we have a little constructive talking time.
jokes.setup: Selina giving an impromptu speech at Catherine's party
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly desperate and incoherent attempt to control the situation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Selina's complete loss of control. The rambling nature captures her desperation.

jokes.text: So do you have any sort of chillaxing kind of music?
jokes.setup: Selina trying to calm party atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Using dated slang 'chillaxing' in professional context
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe from Selina using outdated slang inappropriately.

jokes.text: Monsters. It's like first Princess Diana, now this.
jokes.setup: Bad press coverage of Selina's party behavior
jokes.punchline: Comparing tabloid coverage of Selina to Princess Diana's death
jokes.explanation: Excellent false equivalency showing Mike's delusional PR perspective. The comparison is absurdly disproportionate and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You know, as people say, if it rhymes, it chimes. Who says that? No one says that. I'm pretty sure I heard someone say that.
jokes.setup: Mike explaining the 'Meyer the Liar' problem
jokes.punchline: His invented folksy wisdom immediately called out and his weak defense
jokes.explanation: Great Mike character work - inventing wisdom and immediately getting called out. The exchange structure is excellent.

jokes.text: Yeah, tell me about it. And I'm swimming in it and I'm breathing it in through my snorkel.
jokes.setup: Ben describing the 'Meyer the Liar' story as an 'avalanche of shit'
jokes.punchline: Selina's elaborate metaphor mixing swimming and avalanche imagery
jokes.explanation: Creative extended metaphor that's absurdist but vivid. The snorkel detail makes it funnier by adding specificity to the impossible situation.

jokes.text: Kind of like sitting next to my mother after Thanksgiving dinner.
jokes.setup: Andrew explaining free diving as holding your breath as long as possible
jokes.punchline: Comparing underwater breath-holding to family dinner discomfort
jokes.explanation: Relatable family humor with good economy. The comparison works well and shows Andrew can be charming.

jokes.text: This is not flirting. This is like as subtle as putting a nude photo on Facebook.
jokes.setup: Andrew claiming Selina's behavior is flirting
jokes.punchline: Catherine's modern social media comparison for lack of subtlety
jokes.explanation: Good modern comparison that captures the generational gap. Catherine's perspective on her parents' obvious behavior.

jokes.text: I heard whining and I assumed I needed to apologize.
jokes.setup: Ed asking why Amy is apologizing
jokes.punchline: Her conditioned response to apologize for any male complaint
jokes.explanation: Sharp relationship humor with excellent economy. Shows Amy's awareness of gender dynamics.

jokes.text: Jonah, you're not even a man. You're like an early draft of a man where they just sketched out a giant mangled skeleton, but they didn't have time to add details like pigment or self-respect. You're Frankenstein's monster if his monster was made entirely of dead dicks.
jokes.setup: Jonah's crude behavior and comments about tattoos
jokes.punchline: Amy's elaborate takedown comparing him to incomplete artwork and Frankenstein
jokes.explanation: Absolutely devastating insult with brilliant structure and imagery. The extended metaphor builds perfectly to the Frankenstein punchline. One of the best written insults in TV comedy.

jokes.text: Smile, you stupid asshole. Make it bigger. There you go.
jokes.setup: Selina furious at Andrew but needing to maintain public appearance
jokes.punchline: Coaching him to smile while calling him names
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between public performance and private rage. Shows Selina's political instincts even while furious.

jokes.text: You're a stupid fucking prick. Right? You say it. I'm not gonna say that.
jokes.setup: Selina insulting Andrew while smiling
jokes.punchline: Trying to get Andrew to insult himself
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the fake-smile fight. Selina trying to get Andrew to participate is mildly funny.

jokes.text: Fuck POTUS. Right? And fuck Kent. They're just a bunch of fuckers.
jokes.setup: Learning that POTUS will stall the deal she worked for
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly unhinged profanity about the administration
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate rage but relies heavily on profanity without much wit. Shows her frustration effectively.

jokes.text: Jesus fucking Christ. Here. Take that. Carrot cake's good. You want to try it? Nope. It's all yours. I can use my phone to cut it.
jokes.setup: Catherine sitting alone with her birthday cake after the disaster
jokes.punchline: Gary's awkward attempt to be nice followed by Catherine's modern solution to cake-cutting
jokes.explanation: Bittersweet ending moment. Gary's profane kindness and Catherine's matter-of-fact phone solution capture the episode's tone well.

tmdb_overview: Catherine's 21st birthday party becomes a forum for Selina's budget negotiation with the majority leader, but Amy and Gary are more worried how Selina will handle seeing her ex-husband. Meanwhile, Mike discusses Selina's role in the Uzbek hostage crisis with the press.
insight: Sexual dysfunction plot escalates faster than Selina's composure crumbles.
